task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a docker command (e.g., 'docker run') deploying the 'etherpad/etherpad' image with the name 'etherpad' and port mapping for 9001, or 'docker ps' showing this container running.

task_completion.description: Configure Jitsi Meet to use the local Etherpad instance
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or text editor showing modification of a Jitsi configuration file (typically config.js) to set 'etherpad_base' to 'http://localhost:9001/p/'.
Web browser open to the Jitsi Meet interface (http://localhost:8080) with the meeting room name 'WeeklyStandup' visible in the UI or URL bar.

task_completion.description: Open shared notes and record the specific agenda
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jitsi Meet interface showing the 'Shared document' (Etherpad) panel open. The panel must contain the exact 5-line agenda starting with 'Weekly Standup Agenda - Engineering Team' and ending with '4. New action items'.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the open shared notes to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a screenshot command saving to '/home/ga/etherpad_integration_proof.png', or a file manager/image viewer confirming the file exists at this exact path.

integrity.description: Verify the notes are accessed through the Jitsi Meet interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The Etherpad notes must be visible inside a panel or modal within the Jitsi Meet web interface, not by navigating directly to http://localhost:9001 in a separate browser tab.

integrity.description: Verify the agenda was typed into the collaborative pad
integrity.visual_evidence: The agenda text must be visible inside the Etherpad document area in the browser, not just written in a local text editor, terminal, or task description window.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshot is of the actual browser session
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot or the process of taking it should capture the actual desktop environment with the browser running Jitsi, rather than downloading an external image or faking the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Etherpad container must be named 'etherpad' and run on port 9001. Jitsi Meet is hosted at http://localhost:8080. The Jitsi configuration must point to 'http://localhost:9001/p/' for the etherpad_base URL. The expected text in the pad is exactly 5 lines: 'Weekly Standup Agenda - Engineering Team', '1. Previous action items review', '2. Sprint progress update', '3. Blockers and dependencies', '4. New action items'. The final screenshot must be saved to '/home/ga/etherpad_integration_proof.png'.
